hero.lead: How HobbyHash Coin handles website, wallet, support, mining, SMS, and security information.
hero.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: Privacy policy for HobbyHash Coin and the HOBC web wallet.
meta.title: Privacy Policy
section.changes_to_this_policy.p1: HOBC may update this Privacy Policy as the website, wallet, mining tools, support tools, SMS security, and explorer features change. The updated version will be posted on this page.
section.changes_to_this_policy.title: Changes To This Policy
section.children.p1: HOBC services are not intended for children. Do not use the wallet, support forms, or SMS program if you are not old enough to use cryptocurrency services in your location.
section.children.title: Children
section.contact.p1: For privacy questions, open a support ticket and choose the section that best matches your request.
section.contact.title: Contact
section.cookies_sessions_and_security_lo: HOBC may use session cookies and similar technical storage to keep users logged in, protect forms with CSRF tokens, remember security state, and operate admin and wallet sessions. Blocking cookies may prevent login or wallet features from working.

Security logs may include IP addresses, user-agent strings, timestamps, attempted actions, login results, throttle status, support activity, and admin audit events. These logs help protect users, admins, and wallet funds.
section.custodial_wallet_notice.p1: The HOBC web wallet is custodial. The website controls wallet keys and funds until withdrawal. HOBC also offers an official Windows standalone wallet on the downloads page. Use the Windows standalone wallet or another local wallet for larger balances and long-term storage.
section.custodial_wallet_notice.p2: Wallet records are used to operate the wallet, maintain internal accounting, process deposits and withdrawals, investigate support requests, detect abuse, and maintain security logs. Public blockchain transactions may remain visible on-chain permanently.
section.custodial_wallet_notice.title: Custodial Wallet Notice
section.data_retention.p1: HOBC keeps information as long as needed to operate the website and wallet, maintain security, resolve support tickets, satisfy accounting or legal obligations, audit admin actions, and protect against abuse. Blockchain data may remain public and permanent due to the nature of blockchain networks.
section.data_retention.p2: Support tickets, wallet ledger records, withdrawal records, security logs, and admin audit logs may be retained for operational and security reasons even after an account is inactive.
section.data_retention.title: Data Retention
section.how_we_use_information.p1: Information is used to provide website and wallet access, process deposits and withdrawals, operate support tickets, send ticket notices, protect accounts, verify logins, verify withdrawals when enabled, audit admin actions, prevent abuse, troubleshoot errors, comply with legal obligations, and show honest service status.
section.how_we_use_information.p2: HOBC uses phone numbers and SMS verification data only for account security, login verification, admin verification, support, wallet safety, withdrawal verification when enabled, and service-related notices requested or enabled by the user or administrator.
section.how_we_use_information.p3: HOBC may use aggregated or technical information to understand site reliability, pool status, wallet backend health, explorer sync status, and abuse patterns. HOBC does not use fake balances, fake txids, fake pool stats, fake market values, fake burns, or fake blockchain status.
section.how_we_use_information.title: How We Use Information
section.information_we_collect.p1: HOBC may collect account information such as username, email address, password hash, phone number, phone verification status, account status, security settings, and login timestamps.
section.information_we_collect.p2: HOBC may collect wallet activity needed to operate the custodial wallet, including deposit addresses, withdrawal requests, internal ledger entries, transaction identifiers, confirmation counts, requested destination addresses, request IP address, request browser/user-agent, and wallet support history.
section.information_we_collect.p3: HOBC may collect support ticket details, including requester name, requester email, account ID if logged in, source section, ticket messages, status changes, IP address, browser/user-agent, and admin replies.
section.information_we_collect.p4: HOBC may collect technical and security information such as IP address, browser type, device/user-agent, session identifiers, CSRF events, rate-limit events, failed login attempts, successful login events, SMS challenge records, email delivery events, and admin audit logs.
section.information_we_collect.p5: Blockchain data such as addresses, blocks, transactions, txids, reserve activity, burn activity, and pool-related public mining data may be public by design. Do not submit private keys, seed phrases, passwords, API secrets, or other sensitive secrets through support forms.
section.information_we_collect.title: Information We Collect
section.no_third_party_marketing_sharing: HOBC does not sell, rent, or share personal information, phone numbers, or SMS opt-in data with third parties for marketing or promotional purposes.

HOBC may share limited information only with service providers needed to operate the website, wallet, support system, email delivery, SMS delivery, hosting, database services, security logging, fraud prevention, analytics necessary for operations, or legal compliance. These providers may only use the information to provide those services to HOBC.
section.overview.p1: This Privacy Policy applies to HobbyHash Coin, HOBC, hobbyhashcoin.com, the HOBC command center, public website pages, support forms, the custodial web wallet, mining and pool information pages, explorer/status pages, launch reserve pages, burn tracker pages, email notices, and SMS security messages.
section.overview.p2: HOBC is built around honest status reporting. If data is unavailable, syncing, offline, pending launch, or not available, the site should say that instead of showing fake data.
section.overview.title: Overview
section.sms_and_email_communications.p1: If SMS or email security is enabled, HOBC may send verification codes, ticket notices, and account safety messages. SMS codes are generated by the site, stored as hashes, and expire after a short time.
section.sms_and_email_communications.p2: SMS messages are transactional and security-focused. Message frequency varies based on login attempts, admin verification, verification requests, support activity, wallet security events, withdrawal verification, and account actions.
section.sms_and_email_communications.p3: Message and data rates may apply. Reply STOP to opt out of SMS messages. Reply HELP for help. Opting out may prevent you from receiving SMS login or wallet security codes, which may limit access to protected features when SMS requirements are enabled.
section.user_choices.p1: You may choose not to create a wallet account and still view public pages that are available. You may choose to use the Windows standalone wallet or another local wallet instead of the custodial web wallet. You may request help or account review through support.
section.user_choices.p2: If SMS security is enabled and you opt out or remove your phone number, protected features may stop working until another approved security method is available.
section.user_choices.title: User Choices
